intro: Peripterygia marginata, also known as the New Zealand groundsel, is a perennial plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various skin issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of Peripterygia marginata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Peripterygia marginata is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are commonly used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and minor injuries.
